title: Physiological Signal Processing Pipeline Config
description: Reference configuration schema for EEG, GSR/EDA, HR, fNIRS, and eye-tracking signal pipelines used in VR presence/immersion research. Consolidates conventions from perception-immersion-framework and related literature.

preprocessing_pipeline.step1: Bandpass filter 0.5-45 Hz (removes DC drift and EMG artefacts)
preprocessing_pipeline.step3: ICA artefact rejection (eye blinks, muscle noise)
preprocessing_pipeline.step4: Re-reference to average or linked mastoids
preprocessing_pipeline.step5: Epoch segmentation (1-4 s windows, 50% overlap optional)

band_power.theta: 4-8 Hz — working memory load (increases with CL, esp. frontal midline)

feature_extraction.primary_CL_proxy: alpha/theta power ratio (lower ratio = higher cognitive load)

GSR_EDA.description: Galvanic Skin Response / Electrodermal Activity — sympathetic arousal marker
GSR_EDA.electrode_placement: Non-dominant hand, index and middle finger (volar surface)

SCL.name: Skin Conductance Level (tonic)
SCL.description: Slow-changing baseline; reflects general autonomic arousal

SCR.name: Skin Conductance Response (phasic)
SCR.description: Fast transient peaks time-locked to stimuli; reflects event-locked arousal

preprocessing_pipeline.step3: Decompose into tonic (SCL) and phasic (SCR) components using cvxEDA or Ledalab
preprocessing_pipeline.step4: Baseline correction (subtract mean of 2 s pre-stimulus window)

GSR_EDA.vr_presence_relevance: SCR count and amplitude increase with emotional engagement and surprise events in VR; correlated with IPQ Involvement subscale in several studies

HR_HRV.description: Heart Rate and Heart Rate Variability — autonomic nervous system balance

PPG.note: Lower SNR than ECG; susceptible to motion artefacts in VR locomotion tasks

preprocessing_pipeline.step4: HRV frequency domain analysis (LF: 0.04-0.15 Hz, HF: 0.15-0.4 Hz)

vr_presence_relevance.HR: HR increases with arousal and emotional engagement; decreases with relaxation/flow
vr_presence_relevance.RMSSD: High RMSSD = parasympathetic dominance (calm, flow state); Low RMSSD = stress/high CL
vr_presence_relevance.LF_HF_ratio: Higher ratio indicates sympathetic dominance (stress, high CL)

fNIRS.description: Functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy — hemodynamic response in prefrontal cortex

measured_signals.HbO: Oxygenated haemoglobin — increases with neural activation
measured_signals.HbR: Deoxygenated haemoglobin — typically decreases with activation (inverse to HbO)

fNIRS.roi: Prefrontal cortex (PFC) — BA9, BA46; primary region for working memory and executive function

preprocessing_pipeline.step2: Modified Beer-Lambert Law for HbO/HbR conversion
preprocessing_pipeline.step3: Motion artefact correction (spline interpolation or wavelet)

vr_advantages_over_EEG: Tolerant of movement artefacts — suitable for 6DOF VR
Better spatial resolution than EEG for PFC

eye_tracking.description: Pupillometry and gaze metrics as cognitive/attentional proxies

pupil_dilation.description: Task-Evoked Pupillary Response (TEPR) — strong correlate of cognitive load
pupil_dilation.note: Must control for luminance changes in VR scene

features.fixation_duration: Longer fixations = higher local processing demand
features.saccade_rate: Fewer saccades = higher focused attention or cognitive load
features.gaze_entropy: Higher entropy = less focused visual attention

preprocessing.step1: Pupil diameter baseline correction (subtract mean of 200 ms pre-stimulus)

multimodal_fusion.Q_norm: Normalized questionnaire score (IPQ/SUS mean scaled to [0,1])
multimodal_fusion.P_norm: Normalized physiological proxy (pupil dilation + GSR peaks)
multimodal_fusion.B_norm: Normalized behavioral metric (dwell time + interaction rate)
